units.content: each inference step receives a marker taking one of at least two structurally distinguishable forms based on convergence-status annotation
first form is assigned to steps annotated as converged
second form is assigned to steps annotated as divergent or tension
markers are propagated along edges of a graph where nodes are admissible operational-mechanism identifiers and edges connect co-occurring identifiers within inference steps
marker intensity diminishes by a per-hop decay factor as propagation proceeds along edges
a collision event occurs at a shared operational-mechanism identifier where markers from two or more distinct inference steps coincide with accumulated intensity above a threshold
collision is an opening-collision when all contributing markers are of the first form (converged)
collision is a crack-collision when all contributing markers are of the second form (divergent or tension)
collision is a bridge-collision when contributing markers include both first and second form markers
a collision signal is emitted identifying the shared operational-mechanism identifier, the classification type, and the inference-step identifiers of contributing steps
